LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANTS FAIL TO ADDRESS PLAINTIFF'S CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, PREMISES LIABILITY, AND INVASION OF PRIVACY. DEFENDANT HAS PRESENTED NO RESPONSES TO SPECIFICALLY TARGETED DISCOVERY ALLOWING AN INFERENCE THAT PLAINTIFF NEITHER POSSESSES, NOR REASONABLY CAN OBTAIN, EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT HER CAUSES OF ACTION. SINCE BURDEN HAS NOT SHIFTED, COURT NEED NOT RULE ON PARTIES EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Wayne T. Lamprey, Esq. of Goodin MacBride, Squeri, Day & Lamprey LLP (415) 392-7900 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Stephanie A. Sheridan, Esq. of Sedwick (415) 781-7900 appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendants fail to address plaintiff's causes of action for negligence, premises liability, and invasion of privacy. Defendant has presented no responses to specifically targeted discovery allowing an inference that plaintiff neither possesses, nor reasonably can obtain, evidence to support her causes of action. Since burden has not shifted, court need not rule on parties evidentiary objections. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbbine, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Psychological Exam
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Stephanie A. Sheridan, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Morgans Hotel Group
Ann Hayes Hartman, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Jane Roe

RULING: After Hearing, Defendants MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Psychological Examination is GRANTED with conditions as st forth in the previous tentative, questions limited as set for in order as to deposition, current allegations, prior mental & medical conditions, and no cross-examination as to the allegations regarding incident at hotel. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Pltf'S Motion To Compel Deposition Answers
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Text Ruling

MOTION To Compel Further Deposition Testimony
Off Calendar Feb-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ann Hayes Hartman, Atty. for Plaintiff and moving party: Jane Roe
Stephanie A. Sheridan, Atty. for defendant and opposing party: Morgans Hotel Group

RULING: After Hearing, Defendants MORGANS HOTEL GROUP CO., CLIFT HOLDINGS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony is GRANTED, limited to questions about whether the incident in Mequon took place, questions about what plaintiff alleged in that case, what she subquently alleged in a civil harassment complaint, and whether she recanted those allegations.
